item.title: This post is publick. Remember to wash your mouth after every lick.
item.description:
Its how I feel ever say after my brother death. Its been a year.
There is a movie being made.
I laughed
item.dc:creator: TheReturnOfMadJuan

item.title: Consent is Key
item.description: We have been teaching our kids that they don’t have to give goodnight kisses or goodbye hugs to anyone if they don’t want to. Initially it created some friction with some family members, but the kids have always known that their bodies are their own.
item.dc:creator: dusa1981

item.title: How did it get this bad?
item.description: Back then, posts would hit most popular with 10k+ votes, people would call out obvious strawmen, and people with different views would engage one another. There was rarely a solely political entry to most popular, and way more art, memes, and other random showing. Now, more than half the front page has
item.dc:creator: Pryotra

item.title: Could they look any more villainous?
item.dc:creator: IdUsuallyJustLurkButGeez

item.title: Can’t live with these on my phone anymore meme dump 07
item.description:
**********Trigger warning************** I feel this image. I had to have an abortion this year. And it hurts.
I’m mostly very mad that foreign politics now give ammo to anti-abortionists in my country as well.
Maybe this guy could bash in some skulls.
I got stuck in a post-partum depression, just like after my kids. My emotions have disappeared mostly.
Food is no solution. Bad coping mechanism. Sex is better, but difficult at work.
No dragons at work either.
Hope these memes make someone chuckle.
I have plenty of friends, fortunately.
And cake for breakfast this morning.
Loads to be grateful for.
But I feel like a giant burden on my loved ones.
And like a very horrible mother to my children. PPD has me yelling at them at times, no matter how hard I try.
I’ve got a great husband. That helps a little.
He does our meal planning.
I have no plan for the first time in my life. I feel like it doesn’t matter anymore.
Quitting my job seems like a good idea, but not a smart idea. I’m pretty sure I like my job normally.
The past is haunting me, though. This whole abortion mess brought up a rape from years ago. FML.
Both. Both is good.
The whole society is one big curcus right now.
Getting murdered would be fine. Then it’s not on me when I die. (I’m safe right now. The thoughts are there sometimes.)
Glad to have some puns and memes. A little chuckle every now and then is a spark of hope.
Who would’ve guessed…
Correct!
I need confirmations on all my decisions these days. Depression does that to me.
I’m making this my new picture of the day. Hope it helps.
And I’ll play some switch when my wrist recovers from playing it last week.
Bread rolls.
This is me every day. As an alcoholic this is as strong as I should go.
Distraction ideas are welcome.
I’ll keep hanging in there.
And I’ll pay attention to my sleep schedule.
I’ll forget to laugh at my husband’s jokes.
And maybe change the world a little.
And send memes to my husband. Hope he likes them.
I find his compliments about me being strong difficult.
But I’m happy he keeps giving me support.
And is doing the DM work for our role playing group.
This hit home. It’s difficult to keep looking forward, though.
Or to rewrite my truths in a way that makes them look manageable.
Words don’t always mean much.
I am the most negative one currently. I’ve cut all other toxic ones out already.
My plane is going down. Not sure if we’ll crash and burn.
But I keep trying. For now.
And try not to hate everyone.
I could do with a little less trauma.
And less thinking.
I’ll just keep doing it. Whatever ‘it’ is.
I won’t do that.
I might do this.
Does this count as cat tax?
item.dc:creator: AlmostHappy

item.title: Rights lost are much more difficult to regain.
item.description: Rights lost are much more difficult to regain. When your neighbor loses rights, you must respond as if you are next. YOU ARE! #politics #wtf #seriously_though_wtf #mildly_interesting #women

item.title: Real hero.
item.description: "June begins the war as a fun-loving young woman with a fierce sense of independence that is decades ahead of her time. When the U.S. enters the war she enlists almost immediately, but she is unprepared for the horrors and poor conditions she will face overseas. June battles not only the Nazis, but also hunger, exhaustion, inclement weather and sickness. She often works 18-hour shifts, 7 days a week, caring for the sick, the wounded and the dying. As the war wears on, the daily brutalities tear at her heart, making it harder and harder to keep her fun-loving spirit. But while the constant misery threatens to overwhelm her, June comes to learn that her good-natured attitude is often her most valuable medicine." - History.com
item.dc:creator: dizzyturtle

item.title: Things seen this week during structural assessments!
item.description:
We saw this interesting deck out on one of our assessments. Jenga but 100x bigger!
This home’s access door to their California basement was located in the bathroom. I'm sure they dominated at hide & seek.
Efflorescence is the white chalky powder that you might find on the surface of a concrete or brick wall. It's a sign of moisture intrusion that could lead to major structural issues if not checked out.
Rusted pipes pose a significant threat to your structure’s foundation.
If not taken care of, the post will slip out of place and the girder above will lose crucial support. Regular maintenance on your home is important for this reason. Taking care of the little things now can prevent bigger problems in the future
How close I am to losing my cool.
Rusty pipes soon flake and corrode, creating leaks. Some leaks pose a more significant risk than others.
Leaking pipes can flood the foundation of a building. This could result in cracks in the concrete, significant mold presence, or even the sinking of the foundation. The longer the leaks continue, the greater the damage that they can do to your foundations.
Is it still worth $250,000?
item.dc:creator: AlphaStructural

item.title: It's amazing how you can turn lives around
item.dc:creator: kittylove1993

item.title: Am I the asshole?
item.description: Literally just stoop up and walked away from a co-worker who continued to talk to me (about work) after I politely said I was on my unpaid lunch break, but we could talk after I clock back in. I value my time off from work. Whether that’s my 30 minute lunch, PTO or paid holidays. I’m certainly not here by choice. I’ve been forced back after the company decided Covid wasn’t real anymore (it very much is, but I’d like to stay employed so here I am back in office). Please leave me alone when I’m not getting paid to deal with you. I’ll do the same for you.
item.dc:creator: danrathernot
